reviews.review_title: I have this game because of the Striker
reviews.review_description: This game hails back to the Time Cop 1/2, House of the Dead days, and I flipping love it. I also have the Mavick Striker VR gun, which I think makes this even better. It's well done and continues to challenge. Devs, make more that support the Mavick, please!
Hi I wish the game would have Gun pitch and gun Yaw support so I could use my Ace gun and I give it 5 stars

I've never played the inspiration for Under Cover, (Time Crisis), but I've played several arcade lightgun games from the era and this game nails the experience.

It reminds me of playing Sega's Virtua Cop only in VR and with far better graphics.

The art direction is top notch. There's just enough pixelation in the textures to give a retro look without resorting to super low polygon models. It's a retro style that's easy on the eyes and looks way better than the game that inspired it.

The action is fast and loud, so fast that despite usually preferring to crouch IRL in VR, I switched to the crouch button mode.  Time Crisis used a foot pedal switch so a button seems more appropriate anyway.

I've played through a few chapters so far and they're a good length and a lot of fun.

The game's just a blast to play and super nostalgic!

My only complaint is the letterboxed cutscenes. They would be fine in a flat screen game or in VR in a cinematic fixed screen theater style, but attached to your face they're horrible.  Looking around with your vision restricted to a thin horizontal rectangle is strange and uncomfortable. I have to knock off a star for that.

I suspect that this game is long past getting updates but I dislike the letterboxed cutscenes so much that they could release a paid DLC consisting of nothing but an off switch for this feature and I'd fork over the money. 🤣

Playing this game was a blast! It reminds me of the good old times when I was a kid playing these shooters in the arcades. I would have given it five stars but the only main thing that struggles me while playing is the gun items & health items weren't very placeable to see where they respawn and you cant turn snap angles to where the CPUS are targeting at you.

Hopefully you’ll make a sequel but without all the excessive teleport after teleport after teleport as well as the story or score counter interruptions which ruin the flow. 

Should only need one teleport per scene of enemies, not 5-10. Then you’re listening to the constant unskippable rambling of the characters while you’re stood there doing nothing.

Don’t like the laser pointer air mouse neither that you can’t turn off. 

Unfortunately for me it’s ruined by all the waiting. Giving it a 4 anyway because everything else it does is done right
This is a cool as arcade game with great graphics and a story with it! I hope that the Developers make a sequel to it!
Great light gun game,reminds me of time crisis

reviews.review_title: Very fun, good graphic ,  basic game
reviews.review_description: It’s a fun , basic shooter type of game with easy to learn type format.

It's basic and not worth the full Canadian price of $28.99. 

Arcade shooter of this caliber is basically worth $15, and that's being kind. 

It's entertaining for a bit, but certainly not great. Guns feel pitiful and there's no haptics. No matter the gun they're certainly under powered. 

Not really engaging, very few enemy types, better games out there like crisis brigade.

The only thing I would love to see changed is that after a room is cleared, I’d like to be able to keep shooting things like glass bottles and chandeliers before going to the next level. Maybe have a “shoot the sign” to continue to next level like they have on crisis brigade. And until you are ready to proceed, you can finish shooting glass bottles, barrels ets. The explosions are awesome. This game is soooo fun. I’m 58 year old woman techie who loves rail shooters. I don’t care for the free movement games due to the motion sickness but this one doesn’t have that so it’s fun.
Please consider adding achievements to extend engagement. The game is good. Hopefully further support will increase replayability. Thanks

Really enjoyed playing this game co-op. Quick action, accurate gunplay, multi paths. Scoring system is fun. Wish there were more games like this. It would be great if extra level packs were added, I would pay for them.

reviews.review_title: Game starts me at mission 1 after quit
reviews.review_description: If I stay and play whole game from beginning to end - great, I just keep hitting continue mission after mission, but when I come back into game after quiting to Quest home, the game forgets all my progress and starts me back at mission 1 again. When I return to play later, I want the game to allow me to pickup where I left off at on  mission 4 not start me over at mission 1 and all my progress up to mission 4 is locked again. How do I keep my progress upon returning to game? It is hard to start all over from the start after standing for hours. Game is fun but I want to keep my progress.

developer_response.body: Hi elropapi4sho - We now have pushed a patch live which should resolve problems with saving progress. Please let us know if you're still experiencing this issue.

reviews.review_description: Look if u played time crisis2 and u loved it don't read no more and buy. Whoever saying it's lacking is unrealistic seriously what u want real bullet holes to hit u? U wanna take the controller back on reload? Give me a darn break. 1st thing it's not sandbox right there 5 stars clap clap clap. But u feel every dispursed shot it even clicks like in TC. You can combo shoot everyone of your enemy's! 5 stars clapping. Multi-player 10 stars shooting my gun in the air.💥💥💥it's freaking fun as he'll. Slow motion explosions so u can headshot that guy who hit you ,15 times for a combo. Your a headshot fiend go for it more points u can combo headshots brah. Hit him 4 times make him agree to his death bobbling his head. If there was blood and dismemberment It would be a dream but we can't have it all can we. And for market purposes I get it.but if u like arcade coin or arcade not that chucky cheese David custers stuff but true arcade action get this game it's fun af.$pillDaDrilla

Great game and great concept.  Love that it is coop.
The crouch to reload can be a great workout.

First negative is it always starts you in the tutorial which is annoying and you need to hit the quest button to skip to the main menu.

But the main problem is when playing co-op is a network error that always pops up at some point, then progress does not save.  Have almost made it through the first level a number of times but always hit the error.   This renders the game just unplayable at this point.   Disappointing way to kill the game 

Network errors need to be resolved.   I have a dedicated fiber connection with a high end router in close proximity.

1 star since it is not playable in coop.
Definitely has 4-5 star potential if this gets patched at some point.

developer_response.body:
Hi Sage!

Sorry to hear that you're having this issue. We did push a patch which we thought would resolved this issue, so it's unusual to see it is still occurring. 

Could you confirm you're playing on the latest patch? If possible, would you be able to reinstall the game to see if that resolves the issues you're facing?

If a reinstall doesn't work, let us know and meanwhile we'll continue investigating.

reviews.review_description: Kudos Devs! I'm really scouting this game. Take me back to the arcade days,  only now so much better. I have a few suggestions though.  Can an option to increase difficulty be implemented? Also, can the crosshair be optional? It's easy to break immersion when it keeps showing up. I would even love a manual reloading system, but I know that's too much for an arcade shooter haha! I'm having a great time so far though. That 5th 🌟 awaits. Thanks for a great experience.

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for this lovely review! 

Thank you also for all your suggestions, we will pass these on to the team for consideration. :)

reviews.review_description:
Fusion of nostalgia, newness and VR
It was all a delightful experience for me!
I wish there were more unlockable guns, more challenges, etc.
I'm hoping they'll add it in an update.
My only regret is that there is no quick match!
If anyone wants to play Undercover with me
Send me a friend on Discord:mayuzumikaoru
（I am Japanese. I'm using a translation app to write this text, so sorry if there are any weird parts.）

developer_response.body: Thank you for this lovely review! We're very happy to hear you enjoy the game so much :) Thank you also for your suggestions and feedback, we'll pass it on to the team!

reviews.review_description:
Entertaining game, but I clearly see two things to improve.
1)It needs to have an option to be able to cover itself if we move the jostick down. At first it seems curious to crouch... but after two plays you no longer feel like moving or playing anymore.
2)Cooperative mode has no option to join a random game. Which prevents finding anyone. Only if there are friends to tell the code to, could it be played. But I think it would be nice to have the option to play randomly with other people and help each other.
Extra:
3) This game would do well to have unlockables, to increase the experience bar. To unlock skins/main weapons or modes. I think it's something that would encourage its replayability.
4)The other thing that would improve it a lot would be to have haptic support from bhaptics and provolver.

It's a shame not to be able to choose between several parapets as you progress, but they are all automatic... there is some lack of interaction so as not to tire out the gameplay.

The more you can add, the more stars you will earn.😇
Thanks

developer_response.body:
Hey Nick! Thank you for the review and the suggestions!
Just to advise there is a seated mode so you can play sitting down and not having to "duck" for cover and alternatively you can use "seated mode" while still standing, and press A/X to take cover.
Hope this helps ❤

reviews.review_title: Jagged edges, hard to look at and play
reviews.review_description: I’m considering a refund. It’s not quite Time Crisis. Less punchy, music is not as good and the graphics are bad. How do other games have better anti aliasing than this. Yes it’s meant to have a retro look, but Compound does a much better job, much cleaner retro look. Terrible quality for this price. Sure it reminds you of the arcades but Crisis Vrigade series is better. This game has missed the mark I feel. Shame it could have been good.

it's not bad a little more arcade like than I would like I prefer to move freely around and not teleport but it does have you moving and actually squatting down which is cool. I like the variety of guns and the option to play co-op
This game is very cool and it has so much action also love the weapons that come with the game and the levels are so creative, this game is perfect
Fun light gun arcade style game, reminds me of the gun games for the playstation lots of fun in single player or co op.
It's a very polished VR light gun experience. I would enjoy more content or an endless mode or something, but I really like what is already there.

tiny,cc/y5euwz

Replace , with . for 25% off

From what I can can tell, it’s a game that is true to traditional light gun shooters which is right up my alley!

Cant wait to hop in and beat my high score!
Brought back the member berries of time crisis 2
Slow pace game, maybe good for young kids.

I was super excited about this. And while the game mostly delivers I think a few things really detract from the experience.
1. Why do I have to shoot power ups once, wait, then shoot it again to get it? All gun power ups should function like the health.
2. Too much talking and pauses. Having the big metal gear solid window pop up really breaks the flow of the game. Please add an option to just turn off story and dialog.
3. Let me turn off the target reticle
4. Let me duck with the controllers, either by dropping it down or by pressing a button.
5. The same type of boss for every level?

developer_response.body:
Hey Killz! Thanks for the review! We implemented double shooting on pickups to avoid accidental pickup, which could be annoying if you're using a different one and don't actually want to switch!
Seated mode can be used will standing - press A/X to duck :)!
Hope that helps!

reviews.review_description: I enjoyed playing this game in coop mode with my son, we laughed we shouted we competed. I would buy a dlc or part 2 as long as coop mode is enabled

It was really a pleasant surprise to come across this game. I'm playing it in coop with a friend and everything goes smoothly and without any hitch. Maximum arcade fun that truly reminds you of the golden days of light guns.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review ! So glad you're enjoying the game

reviews.review_title: Good Time Crisis type shooter + 1/4 Off
reviews.review_description:
Really enjoying this. As everyone else is saying, if you remember and enjoyed Time Crisis then you'll enjoy this for sure. 


bio(dot)lin*/ajo998

Replace (dot) with . and the * with a k

Please vote helpful

For me personally, it's too much talkative, too many statistics - interrupting action. More waiting, less gaming.. (counterproductive)
Too much arcadey - rather for kids but some may like CO-OPs. Still, higher pricetag.
I'd prefer dodging bullets, charge gun independently and have fewer but clever/AI enemies and custom difficulty.
If no improvements, I stay with Dead Second and Pistol whip..

For better price, check: tiny,cc/undercovervr  (replace , with . )

I started this up, not expecting too much of it. But after playing the first part of chapter 1, I don't think I'm gonna keep this game.
I like the retro feel and look, but how are some devs able to smooth a lot of edges pretty well, and others have jagged and shimmering edges all over in their games, as does this one.. it's so hard to look at..
Gameplaywise, I missed the feeling of impact, even though you have the exploding barrels and bullet time and such. It is a light-gun inspired game, but the guns and sound effects could have had a bit more oomph. The option to turn off the crosshair is a big miss. And halfway the first part I had a feeling of playing wack-a-mole. Not in the ballpit section, that was kinda funny. But rather the red circle, to alert you of an enemy about to hit you.. holding hands much? I could very well just stand in the open with everyone shooting and missing like Stormtroopers, and just wait until the alerting circle shows up.. it would take even more fun out if it, but you catch my drift. No, it's not gonna keep my attention for long, so I'm just gonna refund. Crisis VRigade 1 and 2 did the light-gun adaptation better and with more depth, sorry..

So much potential here, but it's currently marred by bugs. The recent fix for the save game issue shows the developers care, but I'm still encountering Network Error problems when playing with a friend, causing the game to lock up and require a restart. It's incredibly disappointing to get partway through a level only to have it freeze.

This game could be something special once it's polished. For now, consider it a single-player experience, though it truly shines with a buddy. Hopefully, the developers continue to improve it, as it has the potential to be a hidden gem in the VR space.

Update: Here are my specific issues - I ran into the Network Error when playing two players online. We both have good internet and are on Quest 2 and Quest 3. They could see me in the new section, but I was in the current section. We could not progress though and were forced to quit out of the level, losing all progress at the end of level 2-1. When we tried it again, the same thing happened at the end of level 2-1, except my gun was constantly firing. Maybe if there was a way to reset your placement, or lose all your energy so you respawned in the new spot? It happened at the same place, so that could just be something with that spot.

developer_response.body:
Hi FastShoes,
We're really sorry to hear you're still having issues, but we're dedicated to improving our player experience. Could you please provide us with any of the following information so we can look into what may be causing the problem?

- What hardware you're using while playing
- Any details about your current network connection (speed, ISP etc)
- where are you and your co-op partner located
- how long into gameplay before the issue happened
- any details about what was happening in-game before the issue occurred

Thank you for raising the issue and helping us improve the game!

reviews.review_description: I love shooters this 1 really good
bit. ly/48 g 639O  (remove the spaces)  Good fun in short burst.  Co-op is a definite plus. Good for a couple of play throughs.

reviews.review_description: I started playing this with my 22 years old son, its like Time Crisis and is great in Coop mode

b i t , l y / u n d e r c o v e r 2 5
Fun, but not as good as crisis brigade 2.
Please add protube haptics and bhaptics support 🙏

I enjoy playing VR with my younger siblings and I was excited to play this one with them. But the game has crashed twice and we haven't even beaten the 1st mission.

Crash 1) loading up the 1st mission
Crash 2) game Crash after using the wind-up mini gun... crashed as soon as the level ended.

Edit: increased from 2 to 4 stars as the game hasn't crashed anymore. The game is a bit on the easy side. But definitely a fun game to play co op in.

developer_response.body: Hi there! Thanks so much for this review and your feedback! Great to hear that you enjoyed the co-op mode and were able to enjoy the game with your siblings after all. <3

reviews.review_description:
I've now played for an hour alone and another hour with a friend. I've been looking forward to this title since the first announcement trailer and despite my expectations for this title, they were far exceeded. A love letter to the Time Crisis series, Under Cover takes full advantage of VR, being its own game that only uses mechanics from Namco's templates. It's a fast-paced, action-packed game where you shoot from behind cover and reload your weapons from cover - just like the original. This is more reminiscent of the Police 24/7 arcade machine from Konami, where you also had to actively take cover.
Particularly noteworthy is the co-op mode, which is the main reason for me to buy it and is better than expected because the entire game is designed around it. In addition to the enemies, the entire environment can also be destroyed. There are four missions, each with three sections, although I'm hoping for more supplies here. In addition to the regular fights, the game also includes cool boss fights (unfortunately four times the same) and a motivating high score hunt. I really like the comic-like look and the arcade gameplay. I'm completely thrilled and recommend it to anyone who wants to have a good time with their buddy and can easily play the same game over and over again.
I would just like to see more levels in the future or a new part with more scope and a graphical update like Crisis Vrigade for Crisis Brigade 2 or Time Crisis for Time Crisis 2 and 3 and the possibility of manually reloading while in cover.
What disappoints me, however, is the acoustics. The weapons sound good, but the background music is a bit lame and doesn't really fit the hard-hitting "ACTION!!" and "CLEAR!!" (The German translation is also full of errors) I would have liked arcade, fast and memorable pieces of music. For example, the theme music of Time Crisis is unforgettable. In Under Cover the music would be better suited to a stealth game.
This game also doesn't come close to Crisis Brigade 2. Neither does a Cybercade or Dead Second, although the latter does not have a co-op mode. But these games simply have more oomph and no annoying, constantly chattering characters.You should be able to turn off the dialogues. But there's just more going on here: the enemy waves in Under Cover are usually quite short and you have to wait for long running sequences and dialogues during which you are teleported. So unfortunately the characters don't walk like they do in Time Crisis. This is also a bit disappointing and always slows down the action extremely. Time Crisis is the perfect example how to do it: walking from action to action and the main character remains silent.
But the most important thing is the co-op mode and, as mentioned, it is simply perfect.
Hopefully there will be enough fans and a few skilled modders so that one day there will be a release of all the original Time Crisis titles in VR.

developer_response.body: Thank you so much for the review! So glad you are enjoying the game!

reviews.review_title: Get it if you're grown with Time Crisis
reviews.review_description: I'm a Time Crisis player so I'm gonna be pretty biased since this is by far the closest you can get. However this can be improved by adding press to stand up (instead of always press to hide) and crosshair disabling option.
